@@ -41,6 +41,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","title":"Create 'Starting a New Project' guide section","description":"# Task: Create 'Starting a New Project' Guide Section ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc) ## Description The wizard uses "my-first-project" as an example, but doesn't explain the general pattern for starting real projects. Users need guidance on: - Creating project folders - Initializing git - Setting up with Claude ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx or new /learn/new-project page \`\`\`tsx

Starting a New Project

Ready to build something? Here's the pattern:

1. Create a session for your project

This creates a persistent workspace named "my-awesome-app".

2. Create and navigate to a project folder

3. Start Claude and describe your project

Tell Claude what you want to build. For example:

"Create a React app with TypeScript that shows a todo list"
Claude will set up the project structure, install dependencies, and start building. You can guide it step by step or give it the whole vision at once.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows 3-step pattern (session → folder → Claude) - [ ] Uses concrete example project - [ ] Includes example prompt for Claude - [ ] Explains what Claude will do - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Different color from daily workflow to distinguish - Example project should be relatable (todo app is classic) - Emphasize that Claude does the heavy lifting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:00.569413-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in launch-onboarding with: 3-step pattern (ntm new → mkdir cd → cc), example project 'my-awesome-app', example Claude prompt for React todo app, GuideTip explaining Claude's capabilities, cyan color scheme distinct from daily workflow.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s4cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:33.220094-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","title":"Implement agent auth functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement agent auth functional tests ## Context Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i) ## What to Do Create functional tests for AI agent authentication: ### check_claude_auth() 1. Check if claude binary exists (skip if not) 2. Check if ~/.claude/config.json exists 3. Try claude --print-system-info (low-cost API check) 4. Return: 0=OK, 1=fail, 2=skip ### check_codex_auth() 1. Check if codex binary exists 2. Check for OPENAI_API_KEY env var 3. Check for API key in ~/.zshrc.local 4. Return status ### check_gemini_auth() 1. Check if gemini binary exists 2. Check for GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY 3. Check for credentials file 4. Return status ## Acceptance Criteria - Each check has clear pass/fail criteria - Skipped if tool not installed (status 2) - Auth issues detected reliably - No false positives ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add check functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:18.598224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented check_claude_auth(), check_codex_auth(), check_gemini_auth() with config/API key checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:34.958137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-32g","title":"[ubu.9] Handle LTS-to-LTS upgrade path special case","description":"# Handle LTS-to-LTS Upgrade Path Special Case ## Purpose Ubuntu LTS releases can upgrade directly to the next LTS (22.04 → 24.04) OR follow the regular path through intermediate releases. We need to handle this correctly. ## Background Ubuntu upgrade paths have special rules: - **Normal releases**: Must upgrade sequentially (24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10) - **LTS releases**: Can upgrade directly to next LTS (22.04 → 24.04) - **LTS to normal**: Requires enabling "Prompt=normal" in /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades ## Default Behavior By default, do-release-upgrade on LTS systems only shows LTS upgrades. To see non-LTS upgrades, need to modify configuration: ```bash # In /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades # Change: Prompt=lts # To: Prompt=normal ``` ## Implementation ### Detection ```bash # Check if current system is LTS ubuntu_is_lts() { local version=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) # LTS versions: 22.04, 24.04, 26.04, etc. [[ "$version" =~ ^[0-9]+\.04$ ]] } ``` ### Path Calculation for LTS Systems ```bash ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path() { local target="$1" local current=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) if ubuntu_is_lts; then # Check if we should go LTS-to-LTS or through normal releases local next_lts=$(ubuntu_get_next_lts "$current") if ubuntu_version_gte "$next_lts" "$target"; then # Target is the next LTS or earlier - go direct echo "$next_lts" else # Target is beyond next LTS - need to go through normal releases # Enable normal release channel ubuntu_enable_normal_releases ubuntu_calculate_sequential_path "$current" "$target" fi else ubuntu_calculate_sequential_path "$current" "$target" fi } ``` ### Enable Normal Releases ```bash ubuntu_enable_normal_releases() { local config="/etc/update-manager/release-upgrades" if [[ -f "$config" ]]; then # Backup original cp "$config" "${config}.acfs-backup" # Change Prompt=lts to Prompt=normal sed -i "s/^Prompt=lts$/Prompt=normal/" "$config" log_info "Enabled normal release upgrades (was LTS-only)" fi } ``` ## Example Upgrade Paths ### From 22.04 LTS to 25.10 Option A (LTS path first): - 22.04 → 24.04 (LTS hop) - 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 (normal path) Option B (all normal): - 22.04 → 22.10 → 23.04 → 23.10 → 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 **We choose Option A** - fewer hops, more stable. ### From 24.04 LTS to 25.10 - 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 (normal path, 3 hops) ## Configuration Restoration After reaching target version, restore original LTS-only setting: ```bash ubuntu_restore_lts_only() { local config="/etc/update-manager/release-upgrades" local backup="${config}.acfs-backup" if [[ -f "$backup" ]]; then mv "$backup" "$config" log_info "Restored LTS-only release setting" fi } ``` ## Testing Notes - Test from 22.04: Verify LTS-to-LTS hop works - Test from 24.04: Verify normal path enables correctly - Test config restoration after completion","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:24:05.237039-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:39:24.893274-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:39:24.893274-05:00","close_reason":"Added LTS detection (ubuntu_is_lts - even years only), next LTS calculation (ubuntu_get_next_lts), and release channel management (ubuntu_enable_normal_releases, ubuntu_restore_lts_only).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-32g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:24:40.623353-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-33ug","title":"error_tracking: harden get_error_context_json","description":"scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh was emitting invalid/unsafe JSON in the no-jq fallback (insufficient escaping, empty fields rendered as string 'null') and could break jq mode if LAST_ERROR_CODE is non-numeric (jq --argjson). Harden JSON building to properly escape and emit nulls, and normalize exit_code to a numeric value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:11:59.930522-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:13:55.507176-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:13:55.507176-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in mainline commit a5565e3 (fix(error_tracking): correct JSON fallback encoding)."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","title":"Audit and expand Jargon component terms","description":"# Task: Audit and Expand Jargon Component Terms ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description The Jargon component provides tooltip definitions for technical terms, but coverage is incomplete and some definitions are too technical. Need a comprehensive audit. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/components/jargon.tsx ### Terms to audit/add: - VPS - Virtual Private Server (already exists, verify quality) - SSH - Secure Shell (verify quality) - Terminal - text interface (verify quality) - Command line - same as terminal (alias?) - Root - superuser/admin account - sudo - run as superuser - Ubuntu user - regular user account - tmux - terminal multiplexer - Session - persistent terminal environment - Repository/Repo - code storage folder - Git - version control system - API key - authentication token - Environment variable - system setting - Port - network endpoint - IP address - network address - zsh - shell program - Bash - shell program ### Quality criteria for definitions: - No jargon in the definition itself - One sentence maximum for tooltip - Relatable analogy if possible - Consistent tone ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms listed above have definitions - [ ] Definitions are beginner-friendly (no technical jargon) - [ ] Tooltip text fits comfortably (not too long) - [ ] All wizard pages use Jargon wrappers appropriately - [ ] Mobile tooltip experience is good (tap to show) ## UI/UX Notes - Tooltips should be quick reads (2-3 seconds) - Consider linking to glossary for longer explanations - Test on mobile — touch targets and positioning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:46.425151-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","close_reason":"Added 4 new beginner-friendly jargon terms: session, api-key, environment-variable, clone. All terms include short/long descriptions, analogies, and 'why we use it' explanations. Checked for and avoided duplicates with existing terms."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37sa","title":"Add p10k wizard handling instructions","description":"# Task: Add Powerlevel10k Wizard Handling ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np) ## Description On first zsh login, Powerlevel10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing for beginners. They don't know what it's asking or if they should configure it. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add a GuideTip or AlertCard: \`\`\`tsx Prompt customization wizard? When you first connect, you might see a "Powerlevel10k configuration wizard" asking about fonts and prompt style. You can:
  • Press q to quit and use defaults (recommended for now)
  • Or go through it if you want to customize your prompt appearance

You can always run p10k configure later to customize.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Warns about possible p10k wizard - [ ] Clear instruction to press 'q' to skip - [ ] Mentions ability to configure later - [ ] Non-scary tone (it's a feature, not a problem) ## UI/UX Notes - Use GuideTip (not warning) — this is informational - Keep it brief; most users will just press q - "Recommended for now" guides beginners to the simpler path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:43:03.190685-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","close_reason":"Added a friendly tip about the p10k wizard (press q to skip; p10k configure later)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ci","title":"Fix acfs doctor --json output (pure JSON + correct counts)","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json currently prints section headings before JSON, doesn’t increment pass/warn/fail counters in JSON mode, and doesn’t escape strings. Make --json emit only valid JSON on stdout with correct summary counts (and handle missing /etc/os-release).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:44:19.646755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json to emit pure valid JSON (no headings), increment counters in JSON mode, and JSON-escape strings; also handles missing /etc/os-release."} @@ -62,7 +63,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4rd","title":"Task: Port foundation lessons (0-3) to webapp","description":"# Port Foundation Lessons (0-3) to Webapp ## Parent Feature Lesson Content Pages (cxk) ## Scope Create lesson pages for the first 4 lessons that cover fundamentals: - Lesson 0: Welcome & Overview (`00_welcome.md`) - Lesson 1: Linux Navigation (`01_linux_basics.md`) - Lesson 2: SSH & Persistence (`02_ssh_basics.md`) - Lesson 3: tmux Basics (`03_tmux_basics.md`) ## Why Grouped These lessons share common themes: - All are foundational concepts - All teach terminal/Linux basics - User should complete these before agent-specific lessons - Can use similar component patterns ## Implementation ### Dynamic Route Page ```typescript // app/learn/[slug]/page.tsx import { getLessonBySlug, LESSONS } from '@/lib/lessonProgress'; import { loadLesson } from '@/lib/mdx'; import { LessonSidebar } from '@/components/lesson-sidebar'; import { LessonNav } from '@/components/lesson-nav'; import { LessonContent } from '@/components/lesson-content'; import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'; export async function generateStaticParams() { return LESSONS.map(lesson => ({ slug: lesson.slug })); } export async function generateMetadata({ params }) { const lesson = getLessonBySlug(params.slug); if (!lesson) return {}; return { title: `${lesson.title} | Learning Hub`, description: lesson.description, }; } export default async function LessonPage({ params }) { const lesson = getLessonBySlug(params.slug); if (!lesson) notFound(); const { content } = await loadLesson(lesson.file); return (
); } ``` ### Content Verification For each lesson, verify: 1. Markdown renders correctly 2. Code blocks have syntax highlighting 3. Tables display properly 4. Any images load (if present) 5. Links are valid ### Lesson-Specific Enhancements #### Lesson 0: Welcome & Overview - Add hero section with celebration tone - Include system diagram as interactive component - Link to relevant wizard steps #### Lesson 1: Linux Navigation - Add command examples with copy buttons - Include "Try it" prompts - Consider interactive terminal mockup (future) #### Lesson 2: SSH & Persistence - Link back to wizard Step 6 (SSH Connect) - Emphasize key concepts (public/private keys, fingerprints) - Troubleshooting section #### Lesson 3: tmux Basics - Keyboard shortcut reference table - Diagram of panes/windows/sessions - Link to tmux cheat sheet ## Testing Checklist For each lesson: - [ ] Page renders at `/learn/[slug]` - [ ] Title matches lesson metadata - [ ] Content loads from markdown file - [ ] Code blocks styled and highlighted - [ ] Navigation (prev/next) works - [ ] Mark complete works - [ ] Mobile view is readable ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 4 foundation lessons accessible - [ ] Content matches CLI lesson content - [ ] Consistent styling across lessons - [ ] SEO metadata set for each page ## Dependencies Requires completion of: - Lesson progress tracking (ori) - MDX rendering pipeline (25i) - Lesson layout and navigation (j9a)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:20:57.26599-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.583218-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.583218-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx. No separate porting needed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4rd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.720793-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4rd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-25i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.938628-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4rd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:09.135154-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4xi","title":"Implement confirm_resume() interactive prompt","description":"# Task: Implement confirm_resume() interactive prompt ## Purpose When ACFS detects a previous incomplete installation, ask the user whether to resume or start fresh. ## Critical UX Decision: Silent Resume by Default **Key insight:** Users who run `./install.sh` on a partially-installed system WANT to resume. Forcing an interactive prompt every time creates friction. **Design:** ```bash confirm_resume() { local state_file="$1" local last_phase=$(jq -r ".current_phase_id" "$state_file") local timestamp=$(jq -r ".started_at" "$state_file") # Silent resume by default - just show status log_info "Resuming installation from phase: $last_phase" log_info "Started: $timestamp" # Only prompt if --interactive flag or stdin is a tty AND not --resume if [[ "${ACFS_INTERACTIVE:-}" == "true" ]] && [[ -t 0 ]]; then echo "" echo "Previous installation detected. Options:" echo " [R] Resume from $last_phase (default)" echo " [F] Fresh install (wipe state)" echo " [A] Abort" read -r -p "Choice [R/f/a]: " choice case "${choice,,}" in f) return 1 ;; # Fresh a) exit 0 ;; # Abort *) return 0 ;; # Resume (default) esac fi return 0 # Auto-resume } ``` ## CLI Flags Integration - `--resume`: Force resume without any prompt (explicit intent) - `--force-reinstall`: Force fresh install - `--interactive`: Enable prompts (for manual runs) - No flags + partial state: Silent resume with status message ## Rationale - Agentic workflows run non-interactively; prompts would hang forever - Human users running manually see clear status and can use flags if needed - Matches behavior of `apt upgrade` which just continues by default ## Dependencies - Depends on: state.json schema (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zt) - Depends on: init_state()/save_state() (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Silent resume is the default (no prompts) - [ ] Status message shown: "Resuming from phase X" - [ ] --interactive flag enables prompts for manual users - [ ] --resume flag makes intent explicit - [ ] Works correctly in non-tty environments (pipes, cron, CI)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:43:12.413691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:44:07.526112-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:44:07.526112-05:00","close_reason":"Added confirm_resume() to state.sh with silent resume by default, CLI flag support (--resume, --force-reinstall, --interactive), gum integration, and parse_resume_flags() helper. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4xi","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:28.330237-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4zg","title":"Enforce checksum-verified upstream installer execution","description":"Root cause: checksum verification system exists (scripts/lib/security.sh + checksums.yaml) but many install/update paths still use unverified 'curl | bash/sh'. Integrate security.fetch_and_run into install.sh + scripts/lib/* and update doctor/smoke hints to avoid suggesting unverified installs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T18:50:14.820583-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T19:20:03.20366-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T19:20:03.20366-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced remaining unverified upstream installer execution in scripts/lib/* with checksums.yaml + security.sh verification; tightened security.sh; updated doctor/smoke hints; shellcheck clean; changes pushed.","labels":["installer","security"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4zsi","title":"Harden error_tracking get_error_context_json fallback","description":"scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh:get_error_context_json fallback (no jq) emits invalid/incorrect JSON (strings like "null", incomplete escaping). Update it to properly JSON-escape strings and emit real nulls.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:09:40.856133-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:09:50.635368-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4zsi","title":"Harden error_tracking get_error_context_json fallback","description":"scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh:get_error_context_json fallback (no jq) emits invalid/incorrect JSON (strings like "null", incomplete escaping). Update it to properly JSON-escape strings and emit real nulls.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:09:40.856133-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:12:24.752946-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:12:24.752946-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed get_error_context_json fallback to emit valid JSON (proper escaping + real nulls) and hardened jq path exit_code handling."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-542","title":"Create acfs session list command","description":"# Task: Create acfs session list command ## Context Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb) ## Rationale Before export, users need to see what sessions are available. ## Command ```bash acfs session list # Output: # Recent Sessions (last 30 days): # abc123 2025-01-15 10:30 claude-code 45m "Implemented auth..." # def456 2025-01-14 14:00 codex 20m "Fixed pagination bug" # ghi789 2025-01-13 09:15 claude-code 60m "Refactored API..." # # Use: acfs session export --session-id to export acfs session list --json # JSON array of sessions ``` ## Implementation - Call CASS under the hood - Format output nicely for humans - --json for programmatic use - Show session ID, date, agent, duration, summary ## Acceptance Criteria - Lists recent sessions - Shows key metadata (id, date, agent, duration) - --json works - Graceful error if CASS not installed ## Dependencies Requires: Research CASS format task complete ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/session.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:15.177278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:58.843284-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:58.843284-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented list_sessions function in session.sh: (1) Shows sessions by agent and top workspaces via CASS stats, (2) --json flag outputs structured JSON, (3) --days/--agent/--limit options for filtering, (4) Graceful error if CASS not installed with install link. Also added check_cass_installed() and get_workspace_sessions() helpers.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-542","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eli","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:29.699166-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","title":"Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step","description":"# Task: Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step ## Context Part of EPIC: Pre-Flight Validation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok) ## Rationale Users who skip the wizard and run the installer directly should still get pre-flight validation. Currently they only see failures 15 minutes in. ## Proposal Add preflight as Phase 0 of install.sh: ```bash run_phase 0 "Pre-Flight Validation" run_preflight_checks ``` ### Behavior - Run all preflight checks - On FAIL: Show errors, exit (don't waste time) - On WARN: Show warnings, ask to continue (unless --yes) - On PASS: Continue silently ### Flag ```bash --skip-preflight # Skip pre-flight checks (for debugging) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Preflight runs before Phase 1 - Failures abort immediately with actionable message - Warnings shown but don't block (with --yes) - --skip-preflight available for edge cases ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add Phase 0, add flag - scripts/preflight.sh: Ensure can be sourced, not just executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:00.154616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated preflight into install.sh as Phase 0. Added run_preflight_checks() function, --skip-preflight flag, and auto-download for curl|bash scenario.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.766175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-58by","title":"Refactor install_helpers.sh to remove duplicate feature-flag logic","description":"scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh currently contains duplicated/overlapping implementations for generated-vs-legacy category feature flags (multiple helpers and a later redefinition of acfs_use_generated_category). Consolidate to a single implementation, remove dead code, and add a small shell test to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:08:53.010083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (generated category flag logic consolidated)"}
